title: Chicken Kurkure

ingredients.title: Ingredients (optimized):
items: 250g boneless chicken, cut into thin strips
1 tsp lemon juice or vinegar (for slight tang)
Fresh coriander or chaat masala for garnish

instruction.title: Step 1: Marination
steps: In a bowl, combine chicken strips with:
Coriander powder, Kashmiri chili powder, black pepper, garam masala
Mix well and let it marinate for 30–60 minutes. Yogurt makes the chicken soft inside while keeping the coating crisp.

instruction.title: Step 2: Coating
steps: Add a pinch of salt and chili powder if you want extra heat
Lightly coat each chicken strip. For extra crunch, you can double coat: dip in a little water or yogurt, then coat again in the flour-breadcrumb mix.

instruction.title: Step 3: Frying
steps: Fry chicken strips in batches, ensuring they don’t touch each other.
Fry until golden brown and crispy (~3–4 min per batch).

instruction.title: Step 4: Garnishing & Serving
steps: Sprinkle chaat masala or fresh coriander for added flavor.
Serve hot with mint chutney, spicy mayo, or ketchup.

chefTips: For twisty, crunchy strands (like street Kurkure), use a fork to slightly twist the chicken strips before frying.
Corn flour + all-purpose flour ratio can be 2:1 for the crispiest texture.
Medium-low heat ensures the chicken cooks through without burning the coating.
